item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Due to the changing of image policy, fair use images are no longer accepted due to non-compliance with Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed and, when applicable replaced with text-blocks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really wholesome, compared to most video game skips. I feel sorry for 3085, but that little smile in the last dialogue bubble is sweet as hell. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel a bit sad about 3085. He's a harmless sentient NPC in an MMO & just wants to talk with people, yet his ability to project the thoughts of players interacting with him into the game's dialogue often scares those players away.

+1 for making me feel sad about a self-aware/anomalous RuneScape NPC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Video game skip that ISN'T malevolent? Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
interview log 073 ffs l0l.

Just found this, very happy to see an SCP based off OSRS

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Approved. Would edit in myself but I'm fairly busy IRL right now

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Neat. A big draw of this article is that the object can apparently help recovery memories that have been erased via powerful amnestics. I imagine that in testing this, someone may propose checking if said memory recovery works on victims of SCP-909. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Interestingly, SCP-3085’s memory recall ability appears to be able to bypass all known amnestic drugs currently in use by the Foundation and other organizations. Although SCP-3085 lacks the ability to target and select specific memories from the subject, the memories are generally ones capable of creating a positive response in the context of the conversation. It allows and even encourages those who interact with it to return to it for later conversations; however, after roughly half an hour of conversation, SCP-3085 will refuse to continue interacting with the subject, instead encouraging them to “go play the game”.

To this:

Interestingly, SCP-3085’s memory recall ability appears to be able to bypass all known amnestic drugs currently in use by the Foundation and other organizations.1 Although SCP-3085 lacks the ability to target and select specific memories from the subject, the memories are generally ones capable of creating a positive response in the context of the conversation. It allows and even encourages those who interact with it to return to it for later conversations; however, after roughly half an hour of conversation, SCP-3085 will refuse to continue interacting with the subject, instead encouraging them to “go play the game”.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Whether this ability extends to memories lost to other anomalous objects, such as SCP-909, is under investigation.

item.title: Article soundtrack
item.content:encoded: The song that plays in the area SCP-3085 is in, and the other OSRS song I had in mind while writing it.

item.title: Small image transcripts
item.content:encoded: By popular demand, I have added a footnote with transcripts for dialogue boxes #4 and #8 in the second log.

item.title: [a white '+1' against a low-res blood splatter]
item.content:encoded:
I love everything about this. Nostalgia is a difficult theme to tackle on this site and you've absolutely nailed it. A charming article, made all the more so by the idea of Foundation employees taking a break to play Runescape with it to keep it company and enjoy some old memories.

(Due to this article, I have been listening to the original RuneScape soundtrack for like half an hour now. I would call it 'weaponized nostalgia' but it's remarkably harmless and unmenacing. Well done.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very much agree with calls to include transcripts. I really like the inclusion of the dialogue as it would be seen in game, but I really cannot read the smaller font. I have to no-vote because I feel like it's a good read… I just can't finish it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This brought back fond memories.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never played Runescape but even without a deep knowledge of the game I was able to enjoy this. I enjoyed how reminiscent this is of some old videogame creepastas while taking it in a different direction (helpful entity instead of spooky monster). Only complaint was with the text size in the logs, but other than that this is great.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 from me for the effort put into the interview images and the fun trip down nostalgia lane. I like how this manages to be clearly a love letter to a particular game without shoving too many references in your face or being overt 'RS fanfic'.

However, I'm not sure whether the base idea has enough behind it to grab people who have no interest in RS.

Also, we might have a new contender for cushiest work assignment in the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You went the extra mile with the dialog, this was a really fun read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very, very cool. Glad to see you’re still writing!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering about that too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: then just copy the whole thing instead of posting the link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to protect Pale mans smile.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Divination skill is not present in Old School, only RuneScape 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Believe me, I tried that. The OSRS subreddit auto-removed it because the wiki isn't whitelisted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm.

+ show block
– hide block

Does the fact that 3085 is a memory have anything to do with the Divination skill? For those of you who don't know, a big theme in Runescape as of late is using the manifested memories of gods and the world itself in order to create items and power new technologies.

I've never played OSRS, but the bit about it only being noticed thanks to the rooftop agility course was nice. I need to get back into playing RS soon. Nice skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I play the main version of the game. I didn’t know that they added new agility courses to the old school version.

You should totally post this or at least the link to this on the RuneScape forums. Or did you already? I would be interested in seeing how RS players not familiar with SCP would react to this. It could also attract new people to this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ironman btw

edit: Yeah a couple of things to note;

the last bit with "he likes to stay mysterious" seems a little silly and I'd appreciate it if the dialogue simply ended or it cut off. more of an opinion than anything

like everyone else: perhaps place a text version of the images?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the difficulty in writing them so that they don't sound stupid, I enjoy "creepy video game" as a genre. This is a good entry into that realm. The anomaly is suitably unsettling without getting into narmy attempts to spook, and despite my increasing dissatisfaction with the interview as an exposition device in general, this was done in a satisfactory manner here.

Two nitpicks. I'm not sure it's necessary to specifically mention that it lacks a compulsion effect; out-of-universe I can see why you went out of your way to mention it, but in-universe I'm not sure why that would be specifically noted. And like other posters, I have a little difficulty reading some of the longer dialogue images.

Nice article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly what I came in to say- I'm too amused/impressed by your recreations of the dialogue boxes to tell you to cut them, but I needed to zoom in on my fairly large screen to read a lot of the text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the quality of the dialogue images, this is a great entry!

Have a +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I struggled with trying to figure out the right sizes for the font and such. Guess I can add transcripts later. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I desperately think you need to include transcripts with those logs, but otherwise I'm super into this.

item.title: No matter how many times I leave this site...
item.content:encoded:
…I always come back when I have a new idea.

This is my attempt to tell a digital ghost story while writing a love letter to my favorite game. I hope you like it. There are a few easter eggs in here for OSRS players.

Images are from the game and I claim fair use. Had to imgur hotlink images due to their size; if someone manages to upload them feel free to change image link.
